| ![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image66.png){width="2.134229002624672in" height="2.037542650918635in"}                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| $\overrightarrow{OP} = x\mathbf{i} + y\mathbf{j =}\left( \begin{array}{r}                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| x \\                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| y                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| \end{array} \right)$                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| ![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image67.png){width="3.177658573928259in" height="3.1496062992125986in"}                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| - **Interpret** position vectors                                                                                                                                                             |
+=========================================================+====================================================================================================================================+
| Sketch these position vectors.                                                                                                                                                               |
+---------------------------------------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| a.  $\overrightarrow{OP} = 5\mathbf{i} + 7\mathbf{j}$   |                                                                                                                                    |
|                                                         |                                                                                                                                    |
| b.  $\overrightarrow{OQ} = \left( \begin{array}{r}      |                                                                                                                                    |
|     6 \\                                                |                                                                                                                                    |
|     2                                                   |                                                                                                                                    |
|     \end{array} \right)$                                |                                                                                                                                    |
|                                                         |                                                                                                                                    |
| c.  $\overrightarrow{OR} = - 3\mathbf{i} + 4\mathbf{j}$ |                                                                                                                                    |
|                                                         |                                                                                                                                    |
| d.  $\overrightarrow{OS} = - 5\mathbf{j}$               |                                                                                                                                    |
+---------------------------------------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Write these position vectors in column and component notation.                                                                                                                               |
+---------------------------------------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| a.  $\overrightarrow{OA} =$                             | ![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image68.png){width="3.1770833333333335in" |
|                                                         | height="3.1173611111111112in"}                                                                                                     |
| b.  $\overrightarrow{OB} =$                             |                                                                                                                                    |
|                                                         |                                                                                                                                    |
| c.  $\overrightarrow{OC} =$                             |                                                                                                                                    |
|                                                         |                                                                                                                                    |
| d.  $\overrightarrow{OD} =$                             |                                                                                                                                    |
|                                                         |                                                                                                                                    |
| e.  $\overrightarrow{OE} =$                             |                                                                                                                                    |
+---------------------------------------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| - **Investigation** Point-to-point to position form.                                                                                                            |
+=================================================================================================================================================================+
| We can express any vector using position vectors from the origin using the formula:                                                                             |
|                                                                                                                                                                 |
| $$\overrightarrow{PQ} = \overrightarrow{OQ} - \overrightarrow{OP}$$                                                                                             |
|                                                                                                                                                                 |
| This law is a special application of the parallelogram law of subtraction: $\overrightarrow{AB} - \overrightarrow{AD} = \overrightarrow{DB}$                    |
|                                                                                                                                                                 |
| This formula makes vector calculations easier because we can work with component form.                                                                          |
|                                                                                                                                                                 |
| Consider the following diagram:                                                                                                                                 |
|                                                                                                                                                                 |
| ![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image69.png){width="3.2139654418197727in"                              |
| height="3.1496062992125986in"}                                                                                                                                  |
|                                                                                                                                                                 |
| $\overrightarrow{OP} = \left( \begin{array}{r}                                                                                                                  |
| 2 \\                                                                                                                                                            |
| 5                                                                                                                                                               |
| \end{array} \right)$                                                                                                                                            |
|                                                                                                                                                                 |
| $\overrightarrow{OQ} =$                                                                                                                                         |
|                                                                                                                                                                 |
| $\overrightarrow{PQ} =$                                                                                                                                         |
|                                                                                                                                                                 |
| $\overrightarrow{OQ} - \overrightarrow{OP} =$                                                                                                                   |
|                                                                                                                                                                 |
| Confirm $\overrightarrow{PQ} = \overrightarrow{OQ} - \overrightarrow{OP}$ geometrically.                                                                        |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Let $A(3, - 1)$ and $B( - 5,3)$.                                                                                                                                |
|                                                                                                                                                                 |
| Find $\overrightarrow{AB}$ using $\overrightarrow{AB} = \overrightarrow{OB} - \overrightarrow{OA}.$                                                             |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Given $P(5,2)$ and $Q(6, - 8)$, find $\overrightarrow{PQ}$ and $\overrightarrow{QP}$ using the formula.                                                         |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| $\overrightarrow{DE} = \left( \begin{array}{r}                                                                                                                  |
| 3 \\                                                                                                                                                            |
|  - 4                                                                                                                                                            |
| \end{array} \right).$ Find the coordinates of $E$ if $D$ is $( - 2, - 3)$                                                                                       |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| - **Vector to Position Vector Formula**                                                                                                                         |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| ![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image70.png){width="2.0058967629046367in"                              |
| height="1.9513888888888888in"}![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image71.png){width="2.088888888888889in" |
| height="1.9513888888888888in"}For $P(x,y)$ and $Q(u,v):$                                                                                                        |
|                                                                                                                                                                 |
| $\overrightarrow{PQ} = \overrightarrow{OQ} - \overrightarrow{OP}$\                                                                                              |
| $\overrightarrow{PQ} = \begin{pmatrix}                                                                                                                          |
| u - x \\                                                                                                                                                        |
| v - y                                                                                                                                                           |
| \end{pmatrix}$                                                                                                                                                  |
|                                                                                                                                                                 |
| Also:                                                                                                                                                           |
|                                                                                                                                                                 |
| $$\ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \overrightarrow{PQ} = \overrightarrow{PO} + \overrightarrow{OQ}$$                                                                         |
|                                                                                                                                                                 |
| - Always check your work with a sketch.                                                                                                                         |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| - **Example** Apply the formula $\overrightarrow{PQ} = \overrightarrow{OQ} - \overrightarrow{OP}$                                  |
+====================================================================================================================================+
| ![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image72.png){width="2.4208333333333334in" |
| height="2.3645833333333335in"}Given $A( - 4,5)$ and $B(3, - 1)$, find the magnitude and direction of $\overrightarrow{BA},$\       |
| giving your answer in exact form for magnitude and as an angle of inclination to 1 d.p.                                            |
|                                                                                                                                    |
| $$\overrightarrow{BA} = \overrightarrow{OA} - \overrightarrow{OB}$$                                                                |
|                                                                                                                                    |
| $$= \left( \begin{array}{r}                                                                                                        |
|  - 4 \\                                                                                                                            |
| 5                                                                                                                                  |
| \end{array} \right) - \left( \begin{array}{r}                                                                                      |
| 3 \\                                                                                                                               |
|  - 1                                                                                                                               |
| \end{array} \right)$$                                                                                                              |
|                                                                                                                                    |
| $$= \left( \begin{array}{r}                                                                                                        |
|  - 7 \\                                                                                                                            |
| 6                                                                                                                                  |
| \end{array} \right)$$                                                                                                              |
|                                                                                                                                    |
| $$\left| \overrightarrow{BA} \right| = \sqrt{( - 7)^{2} + 6^{2}} = \sqrt{85}\ $$                                                   |
|                                                                                                                                    |
| $$A = \tan^{- 1}\left( \frac{6}{7} \right) = 40.6{^\circ}$$                                                                        |
|                                                                                                                                    |
| $$\theta = 180 - 40.6{^\circ} = 139.4{^\circ}$$                                                                                    |
|                                                                                                                                    |
| Check your answer using a sketch.                                                                                                  |
+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| - **Guided Practice**                                                                                                              |
+====================================================================================================================================+
| ![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image72.png){width="2.4208333333333334in" |
| height="2.3645833333333335in"}Given $\overrightarrow{OD} = - 8\mathbf{i} + 2\mathbf{j}$ and $\overrightarrow{OE} = - 5\mathbf{j}$, |
| find the magnitude and direction of $\overrightarrow{DE}$,\                                                                        |
| giving your answer in exact form for magnitude and as an angle of inclination to 1 d.p.                                            |
|                                                                                                                                    |
| $\sqrt{113}$ and $\theta = 318.8{^\circ}$                                                                                          |
+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

Foundation

1.  Write each vector $\overrightarrow{AB}$ using vector notation and
    draw the equivalent position vector.

+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| a.  ![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image73.png){width="2.316019247594051in" | b.  ![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image73.png){width="2.31123031496063in"  | c.  ![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image73.png){width="2.31123031496063in" |
|     height="2.123438320209974in"}                                                                                                     |     height="2.119047462817148in"}                                                                                                     |     height="2.119047462817148in"}                                                                                                    |
|                                                                                                                                       |                                                                                                                                       |                                                                                                                                      |
| $$\left( \begin{array}{r}                                                                                                             | $$\left( \begin{array}{r}                                                                                                             | $$\left( \begin{array}{r}                                                                                                            |
| 3 \\                                                                                                                                  | 4 \\                                                                                                                                  |  - 2 \\                                                                                                                              |
| 2                                                                                                                                     | 1                                                                                                                                     | 4                                                                                                                                    |
| \end{array} \right)$$                                                                                                                 | \end{array} \right)$$                                                                                                                 | \end{array} \right)$$                                                                                                                |

| d.  ![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image73.png){width="2.31123031496063in"  | e.  ![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image73.png){width="2.311111111111111in" |                                                                                                                                      |
|     height="2.119047462817148in"}                                                                                                     |     height="2.11875in"}                                                                                                               |                                                                                                                                      |
|                                                                                                                                       |                                                                                                                                       |                                                                                                                                      |
| $$\left( \begin{array}{r}                                                                                                             | $$\left( \begin{array}{r}                                                                                                             |                                                                                                                                      |
| 5 \\                                                                                                                                  |  - 2 \\                                                                                                                               |                                                                                                                                      |
|  - 5                                                                                                                                  |  - 1                                                                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                      |
| \end{array} \right)$$                                                                                                                 | \end{array} \right)$$                                                                                                                 |                                                                                                                                      |
+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

2.  On the diagram, show position vectors representing:

    a.  ![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image74.png){width="1.9519805336832896in"
        height="1.9563779527559055in"}![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image75.png){width="1.968503937007874in"
        height="1.9563779527559055in"}$2\overset{̰}{a}\  + \ 3\overset{̰}{b}$

    b.  $3\overset{̰}{a}\  - \ \overset{̰}{b}$

    c.  $- 2\overset{̰}{a}\  - \ 2\overset{̰}{b}$

3.  Given $\overrightarrow{OA}\  = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
     - 3 \\
    4
    \end{array} \right)$ and
    $\overrightarrow{OB}\  = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
    2 \\
    1
    \end{array} \right),$ find the resultant vector for:

+-----------------------------------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------+
| a.  $\overrightarrow{OA}\  + \ \overrightarrow{OB}$ | b.  $\ \overrightarrow{OB}\  - \ \overrightarrow{OA}$ | c.  $\overrightarrow{OA}\  - \ \overrightarrow{OB}$ |
|                                                     |                                                       |                                                     |
| $$\left( \begin{array}{r}                           | $$\left( \begin{array}{r}                             | $$\left( \begin{array}{r}                           |
|  - 1 \\                                             | 5 \\                                                  |  - 5 \\                                             |
| 5                                                   |  - 3                                                  | 3                                                   |
| \end{array} \right)$$                               | \end{array} \right)$$                                 | \end{array} \right)$$                               |
+=====================================================+=======================================================+=====================================================+

Development

4.  If $\overrightarrow{OX}\  = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
    2 \\
    5
    \end{array} \right)$ and
    $\overrightarrow{OY}\  = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
     - 4 \\
    3
    \end{array} \right),$ find the resultant vector for:

+------------------------------------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------+
| a.  $\overrightarrow{OX}\  + \ \overrightarrow{OY}\$ | b.  $\overrightarrow{OY}\  - \ \overrightarrow{OX}$ |
|                                                      |                                                     |
| $$\left( \begin{array}{r}                            | $$\left( \begin{array}{r}                           |
|  - 2 \\                                              |  - 6 \\                                             |
| 8                                                    |  - 2                                                |
| \end{array} \right)$$                                | \end{array} \right)$$                               |
+======================================================+=====================================================+

5.  Given $\overrightarrow{OA}\  = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
    5 \\
    2
    \end{array} \right)$ and
    $\overrightarrow{OB}\  = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
    1 \\
    1
    \end{array} \right)$, find the magnitude and direction of:\
    Draw a sketch to check your answer.

+---------------------------+-----------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------+
| a.  $\overrightarrow{AB}$ | b.  $\ \overrightarrow{BA}$ | c.  $\overrightarrow{OA}\  + \ \overrightarrow{OB}$ |
|                           |                             |                                                     |
| $$4.1,\ 194{^\circ}$$     | $$4.1,\ 14{^\circ}$$        | $$6.7,\ 27{^\circ}$$                                |
+===========================+=============================+=====================================================+

6.  Given $\overrightarrow{OX}\  = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
     - 2 \\
    1
    \end{array} \right)$ and
    $\overrightarrow{OY}\  = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
    8 \\
     - 3
    \end{array} \right),$ find the magnitude and direction of:\
    Draw a sketch to check your answer.

+---------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------+
| a.  $\overrightarrow{XY}$ | b.  $\overrightarrow{OX}\  + \ \overrightarrow{OY}$ | c.  $\overrightarrow{OX}\  - \ \overrightarrow{OY}$ |
|                           |                                                     |                                                     |
| $$10.8,\ 338{^\circ}$$    | $$6.3,\ 342{^\circ}$$                               | $$10.8,\ 158{^\circ}$$                              |
+===========================+=====================================================+=====================================================+

7.  Given $\overrightarrow{OA}\  = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
    a \\
    b
    \end{array} \right)$ and
    $\overrightarrow{OB}\  = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
     - 1 \\
     - 3
    \end{array} \right),$ the vector for $\overrightarrow{AB}$ is
    $\left( \begin{array}{r}
    7 \\
    3
    \end{array} \right).$\
    Find the values of $a$ and $b$.

$$a\  = \  - 8,\ b\  = \  - 6$$

8.  Suppose that $P$ is the point (4, -1), $Q$ is the point (-3, 5) and
    $O$ is the origin.\
    Write as column vectors:

    a.  $\overrightarrow{OP}$

$$\overrightarrow{OP}\  = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
4 \\
 - 1
\end{array} \right)$$

b.  $2\overrightarrow{OP}\  - \ \overrightarrow{OQ}\$

$${2\overrightarrow{(OP)}\  = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
8 \\
 - 2
\end{array} \right)\ \ 
}{\overrightarrow{(OQ)}\  = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
 - 3 \\
5
\end{array} \right)
}{2\overrightarrow{OP}\  - \ \overrightarrow{OQ}\  = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
8\  - \ ( - 3) \\
 - 2\  - \ 5
\end{array} \right) = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
11 \\
 - 7
\end{array} \right)}$$

c.  $\overrightarrow{PQ}$

$${\overrightarrow{PQ}\  = \ \overrightarrow{OQ}\  - \ \overrightarrow{OP}
}{= \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
 - 3\  - \ 4 \\
5\  - \ ( - 1)
\end{array} \right)
}{= \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
 - 7 \\
6
\end{array} \right)}$$

d.  $\overrightarrow{QP}$

$${\overrightarrow{QP}\  = \ \overrightarrow{OP}\  - \ \overrightarrow{OQ}
}{= \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
4\  - \ ( - 3) \\
( - 1) - \ 5
\end{array} \right)
}{= \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
7 \\
 - 6
\end{array} \right)}$$

9.  If A and B are the points (1, -1) and (7, 3) respectively, find:

    a.  $\overrightarrow{AB}$ in component form,

$${\underset{\sim}{a} = \ \overrightarrow{OA}\  = \ \mathbf{i}\  - \ \mathbf{j}
}{\underset{\sim}{b} = \ \overrightarrow{OB}\  = \ 7\mathbf{i}\  + \ 3\mathbf{j}
}{\overrightarrow{AB}\  = \ \overrightarrow{OB}\  - \ \overrightarrow{OA}
}{= \ (7\mathbf{i}\  + \ 3\mathbf{j})\  - \ \left( \mathbf{i}\  - \ \mathbf{j} \right)
}{= \ 6\mathbf{i}\  + \ 4\mathbf{j}}$$

b.  $|\overrightarrow{AB}|,$

$${\left| \overrightarrow{AB} \right| = \ \sqrt{6^{2} + \ 4^{2}}
}{= \ 2\sqrt{}13}$$

c.  the unit vector in the direction of $\overrightarrow{AB}$.

$${\widehat{AB}\  = \frac{\overrightarrow{AB}}{\left| \overrightarrow{AB} \right|}
}{= \frac{6\mathbf{i}\  + \ 4\mathbf{j}}{2\sqrt{13}}
}{= \frac{3}{\sqrt{13}}\mathbf{i}\  + \frac{2}{\sqrt{13}}\mathbf{j}}$$

10. Given three points A, B, and C with position vectors
    $\left( \begin{array}{r}
    2 \\
    3
    \end{array} \right),\ \left( \begin{array}{r}
    5 \\
    7
    \end{array} \right),$ and $\left( \begin{array}{r}
    8 \\
    k
    \end{array} \right)$ respectively, find the value of $k$ such that
    $\overrightarrow{AB}$ is parallel to $\overrightarrow{BC}$.

$${\overrightarrow{AB}\  = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
5 \\
7
\end{array} \right) - \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
2 \\
3
\end{array} \right) = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
3 \\
4
\end{array} \right)
}{\overrightarrow{BC}\  = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
8 \\
k
\end{array} \right) - \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
5 \\
7
\end{array} \right) = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
3 \\
k - 7
\end{array} \right)
}{For\ parallel:\ k - 7\  = \ 4,\ 
}{k\  = \ 11}$$

# Solving Geometric Problems with Vectors

+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| - **Vector Laws**                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
+====================================================================================================================================+===================================================================================================================================+
| The following laws are useful for solving geometrical problems:                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| **Commutative Law**                                                                                                                | **Negative Vector Law**                                                                                                           |
|                                                                                                                                    |                                                                                                                                   |
| $$\overset{̰}{a} + \overset{̰}{b} = \overset{̰}{b} + \overset{̰}{a}$$                                                                  | $$\overrightarrow{AB} = - \overrightarrow{BA}\mathbf{\ }$$                                                                        |
|                                                                                                                                    |                                                                                                                                   |
| ![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image42.png){width="1.847561242344707in"  | ![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image43.png){width="2.869812992125984in" |
| height="1.230596019247594in"}                                                                                                      | height="1.445161854768154in"}                                                                                                     |
+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| **Triangle Law of Addition**                                                                                                       | **Parallelogram Law of Subtraction**                                                                                              |
|                                                                                                                                    |                                                                                                                                   |
| $$\overrightarrow{AB} + \overrightarrow{BC} = \overrightarrow{AC}$$                                                                | $$\overrightarrow{AB} - \overrightarrow{AD} = \overrightarrow{DB}$$                                                               |
|                                                                                                                                    |                                                                                                                                   |
| ![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image44.png){width="1.4949267279090113in" | ![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image45.png){width="2.361111111111111in" |
| height="1.3497265966754155in"}                                                                                                     | height="1.1722222222222223in"}                                                                                                    |
+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| **Vector to Position Vector Formula**                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| $\overrightarrow{PQ} = \overrightarrow{OQ} - \overrightarrow{OP}$, where $O$ is the origin                                                                                                                                                                             |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| ![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image70.png){width="2.0058967629046367in" height="1.9513888888888888in"}                                                                                                      |
+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| - **Example** Solve geometric problems involving vectors.                                                                                                                                                                                              |
+===================================================================+=============================================================+======================================================================================================================+
| ![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image76.png){width="2.4375in" height="1.4414873140857394in"}PQR is a triangle. $M$ is the midpoint of $QR$.                                                   |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| Let $\overrightarrow{PR} = \overset{̰}{a}$ and $\overrightarrow{PQ} = \overset{̰}{b}$.                                                                                                                                                                   |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| Find in terms of $\overset{̰}{a}$ and $\overset{̰}{b}$:                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
+-------------------------------------------------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| a.  $\overrightarrow{QR}$                                         | b.  $\overrightarrow{QM}$                                   | c.  $\overrightarrow{PM}$                                                                                            |
|                                                                   |                                                             |                                                                                                                      |
| $\overrightarrow{QR} = \overrightarrow{PR} - \overrightarrow{PQ}$ | $\overrightarrow{QM} = \frac{1}{2}\overrightarrow{QR}$      | $\overrightarrow{PM} = \overrightarrow{PQ} + \overrightarrow{QM}$ (△ law)                                            |
| (▱ law)                                                           |                                                             |                                                                                                                      |
|                                                                   | $= \frac{1}{2}\left( \overset{̰}{a} - \overset{̰}{b} \right)$ | $$\ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \  = \overset{̰}{b} + \frac{1}{2}\left( \overset{̰}{a} - \overset{̰}{b} \right)$$ |
| $= \overset{̰}{a} - \overset{̰}{b}$                                 |                                                             |                                                                                                                      |
|                                                                   |                                                             | $$\ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \  = \frac{1}{2}\overset{̰}{a} + \frac{1}{2}\overset{̰}{b}$$                     |
+-------------------------------------------------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| - **Guided Practice**                                                                                                                       |
+==============================================+==============================================+===============================================+
| ![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image77.png){width="2.9536231408573927in"          |
| height="1.358620953630796in"}ABCD is a parallelogram. $X$ is the midpoint of $BC$ and the lengths $DY:YC$ are in the ratio 2:1.             |
|                                                                                                                                             |
| Let $\overrightarrow{AD} = \overset{̰}{d}$ and $\overrightarrow{AB} = \overset{̰}{b}.$                                                        |
|                                                                                                                                             |
| Show that $\overrightarrow{XY} = \frac{1}{2}\left( \overset{̰}{d} - \frac{2}{3}\overset{̰}{b} \right)$                                        |
+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| ![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image78.png){width="2.3017891513560804in"          |
| height="1.5397911198600176in"}ABC is a triangle. The point $P$ lies on line $BC$ such that $BP:PC = 1:2$.                                   |
|                                                                                                                                             |
| It is given that $\overrightarrow{BA} = \overset{̰}{a} - 4\overset{̰}{b}\ and\ \overrightarrow{AC} = 3\overset{̰}{a} + \overset{̰}{b}.$         |
|                                                                                                                                             |
| Find in terms of $\overset{̰}{a}$ and $\overset{̰}{b}$:                                                                                       |
+----------------------------------------------+----------------------------------------------+-----------------------------------------------+
| a.  $\overrightarrow{BC}$                    | b.  $\overrightarrow{BP}$                    | c.  $\overrightarrow{AP}$                     |
|                                              |                                              |                                               |
| $$4\overset{̰}{a} - 3\overset{̰}{b}$$          | $$\frac{4}{3}\overset{̰}{a} - \overset{̰}{b}$$ | $$\frac{1}{3}\overset{̰}{a} + 3\overset{̰}{b}$$ |
+----------------------------------------------+----------------------------------------------+-----------------------------------------------+
| - **Parallel Vectors**                                                                                                                      |
+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Two vectors $\overset{̰}{a}$ and $\overset{̰}{b}$ are **parallel** if they have the **same direction** or **opposite directions.**            |
|                                                                                                                                             |
| In other words, one vector is a scalar multiple of the other.                                                                               |
|                                                                                                                                             |
| ![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image79.png){width="4.548611111111111in"           |
| height="1.4125in"}                                                                                                                          |
|                                                                                                                                             |
| $$\overset{̰}{b} = \lambda\overset{̰}{a},\ \ \lambda\mathbb{\in R}$$                                                                          |
+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| - **Example** Solve geometric problems involving vectors.                                         |
+===================================================================================================+
| $\overrightarrow{AB} = \overset{̰}{a} + 2\overset{̰}{b}$ and                                        |
| $\overrightarrow{BC} = \overset{̰}{a} - 4\overset{̰}{b}$.                                           |
|                                                                                                   |
| Show that $\overrightarrow{AC}$ is parallel to $\overset{̰}{a} - \overset{̰}{b}$.                   |
|                                                                                                   |
| $\overrightarrow{AC} = \overrightarrow{AB} + \overrightarrow{BC}$ (△ law)                         |
|                                                                                                   |
| $= \left( \overset{̰}{a} + 2\overset{̰}{b} \right) + \left( \overset{̰}{a} - 4\overset{̰}{b} \right)$ |
|                                                                                                   |
| $= 2\overset{̰}{a} - 2\overset{̰}{b}$                                                               |
|                                                                                                   |
| $= 2\left( a - \overset{̰}{b} \right)$ $\therefore$ parallel to $\overset{̰}{a} - \overset{̰}{b}$    |
+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| - **Guided Practice**                                                                                                              |
+====================================================================================================================================+
| $\overrightarrow{AB} = 2\overset{̰}{a} + \overset{̰}{b}$ and $\overrightarrow{BC} = 2\overset{̰}{a} - 3\overset{̰}{b}$.                |
|                                                                                                                                    |
| Show that $\overrightarrow{AC}$ is parallel to $2\overset{̰}{a} - \overset{̰}{b}$.                                                   |
+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| $\overrightarrow{PQ} = 6\overset{̰}{a} + \overset{̰}{b}$ and $\overrightarrow{RQ} = 2\overset{̰}{a} + 5\overset{̰}{b}$.                |
|                                                                                                                                    |
| Show that $\overrightarrow{PR}$ is parallel to $\overset{̰}{b} - \overset{̰}{a}$.                                                    |
+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| - **Collinear**                                                                                                                    |
+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Three (or more) points are **collinear** if they all lie on the same straight line.                                                |
|                                                                                                                                    |
| ![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image80.png){width="1.5798611111111112in" |
| height="1.3420898950131233in"}Three points A, B, C are collinear if the **vectors connecting them** are **parallel** and **share a |
| common point**.                                                                                                                    |
|                                                                                                                                    |
| - The vectors $\overrightarrow{AB}$ and $\overrightarrow{BC}$ are parallel                                                         |
|                                                                                                                                    |
| - (or vectors $\overrightarrow{AB}$ and $\overrightarrow{AC}$ are parallel).                                                       |
+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

+-------------------------------------------------------------------+
| - **Example** Solve geometric problems involving vectors.         |
+===================================================================+
| $\overrightarrow{AC} = 4\overset{̰}{a} - 8\overset{̰}{b}$ and       |
| $\overrightarrow{BC} = 6\overset{̰}{a} - 12\overset{̰}{b}$.         |
|                                                                   |
| Prove that points $A,B,C$ are collinear.                          |
|                                                                   |
| $\overrightarrow{AC} = 4\overset{̰}{a} - 8\overset{̰}{b}$           |
| $\overrightarrow{BC} = 6\overset{̰}{a} - 12\overset{̰}{b}$          |
|                                                                   |
| $= 4\left( \overset{̰}{a} - 2\overset{̰}{b} \right)$                |
| $\ \ \ \ \ \ \  = 6\left( \overset{̰}{a} - 2\overset{̰}{b} \right)$ |
|                                                                   |
| $\overrightarrow{AC}$ and $\overrightarrow{BC}$ are parallel and  |
| pass through common point $C$, $\therefore$ parallel.             |
+-------------------------------------------------------------------+

+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| - **Guided Practice**                                                                                                             |
+===================================================================================================================================+
| $\overrightarrow{PQ} = 3\overset{̰}{p} + 9\overset{̰}{q}$ and $\overrightarrow{PR} = 5\overset{̰}{p} + 15\overset{̰}{q}$.             |
|                                                                                                                                   |
| Prove that points $P,Q,R$ are collinear.                                                                                          |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| ![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image81.png){width="2.189583333333333in" |
| height="2.1002121609798774in"}The diagram shows congruent parallelograms.                                                         |
|                                                                                                                                   |
| Let $\overrightarrow{BE} = \overset{̰}{a}$ and $\overrightarrow{BC} = \overset{̰}{b}$.                                              |
|                                                                                                                                   |
| Prove that $A,E,I$ lie on a straight line.                                                                                        |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

Foundation

1.  Let $\underset{\sim}{a},\underset{\sim}{b}$ and $\underset{\sim}{m}$
    be the respective position vectors representing the points
    $A(4,\  - 7),\ B(6,\ 3)$ and $M$, where $M$ is the midpoint of $AB$.
    Write in component form:

    a.  $\underset{\sim}{a}$

$$4\mathbf{i} - 7\mathbf{j}$$

b.  $\underset{\sim}{b}$

$$6\mathbf{i} + 3\mathbf{j}$$

c.  $\underset{\sim}{m}\$

$${M = \left( \frac{4 + 6}{2},\frac{- 7 + 3}{2} \right)
}{= (5, - 2)
}{= 5\mathbf{i} - 2\mathbf{j}}$$

2.  ![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image82.png){width="7.046734470691163in"
    height="1.4012346894138232in"}Write the vectors
    $\overrightarrow{AC}$ in terms of $\underset{\sim}{a}$ and
    $\underset{\sim}{b}$.

+---------------------------------------------+---------------------------------------------+-----------------------------------------------+
| a.                                          | b.                                          | c.                                            |
|                                             |                                             |                                               |
| $$\underset{\sim}{a} + \underset{\sim}{b}$$ | $$\underset{\sim}{a} - \underset{\sim}{b}$$ | $$- \underset{\sim}{a} - \underset{\sim}{b}$$ |
+=============================================+=============================================+===============================================+

3.  PQR is a triangle. $\overrightarrow{PR}\  = 2\underset{\sim}{a}$
    and$\ \overrightarrow{RQ}\  = \underset{\sim}{b}$.

Write in terms of $\underset{\sim}{a}$ and $\underset{\sim}{b}:$

![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image83.png){width="2.817361111111111in"
height="1.8229166666666667in"}

a.  $\overrightarrow{PQ}$

> $\underset{\sim}{2a} + \underset{\sim}{b}$

b.  $\overrightarrow{QP}$

> $$- \underset{\sim}{2a} - \underset{\sim}{b}$$

4.  ABC is a triangle.
    $\overrightarrow{AB} = 2\underset{\sim}{a} + \underset{\sim}{b}$ and
    $\overrightarrow{BC}\  = \underset{\sim}{a}\  - 3\underset{\sim}{b}.$

![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image84.png){width="2.3174595363079615in"
height="1.95080271216098in"}Write in terms of $\underset{\sim}{a}$ and
$\underset{\sim}{b}$:

a.  $\overrightarrow{AC}$

> $\underset{\sim}{3a} - \underset{\sim}{2b}$

b.  $\overrightarrow{CA}$

> $$- \underset{\sim}{3a} + \underset{\sim}{2b}$$

5.  LMN is a triangle. $\overrightarrow{ML}\  = 3\underset{\sim}{a}$ and
    $\overrightarrow{NL}\  = \underset{\sim}{a} + \underset{\sim}{b}.$

> ![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image85.png){width="2.159420384951881in"
> height="1.3511679790026248in"}Write in terms of $\underset{\sim}{a}$
> and $\underset{\sim}{b}:$

a.  $\overrightarrow{LM}$

> $$- 3\underset{\sim}{a}$$

b.  $\overrightarrow{NM}$

> $$- 2\underset{\sim}{a} + \underset{\sim}{b}$$

6.  ![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image86.png){width="3.165168416447944in"
    height="2.0231452318460192in"}Here is the quadrilateral ABCD.

    a.  Write the vector $\overrightarrow{AC}$ in terms of
        $\underset{\sim}{n}$ and $\underset{\sim}{o}$.

$\underset{\sim}{n} - \underset{\sim}{o}$

b.  Write the vector $\overrightarrow{AC}$ in terms of
    $\underset{\sim}{m}$ and $\underset{\sim}{p}$.

$\underset{\sim}{m} - \underset{\sim}{p}$

c.  Complete these vector journeys for $\overrightarrow{DB}$.

    i.  $\overrightarrow{DB}\  = \ \overrightarrow{DA}\  + \ \overrightarrow{AB}\  =$
        ...............

$$- \underset{\sim}{m} + \underset{\sim}{n}$$

ii. $\overrightarrow{DB}\  = \ \overrightarrow{DC}\  + \ \overrightarrow{CB}\  = \$...............

$$- \underset{\sim}{p} + \underset{\sim}{o}$$

7.  $WXYZ$ is a quadrilateral.

![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image87.png){width="2.8430555555555554in"
height="1.7680555555555555in"}Write the vector $\overrightarrow{WZ}\$in
terms of $\underset{\sim}{a}$ and $\underset{\sim}{b}$.

$$\underset{\sim}{a} + \underset{\sim}{2b}$$

8.  $ABC$ is a triangle. The point $M$ is the midpoint of $BC$.

> ![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image88.png){width="2.569698162729659in"
> height="1.2701148293963254in"}Write the vectors in terms of
> $\underset{\sim}{a}$ and $\underset{\sim}{b}$.

a.  $\overrightarrow{BC}$

> $$\underset{\sim}{a} + \underset{\sim}{b}$$

b.  $\overrightarrow{BM}$

> $$\left( \frac{1}{2} \right)\underset{\sim}{a} + \left( \frac{1}{2} \right)\underset{\sim}{b}$$

c.  $\overrightarrow{AM}$

> $$- \left( \frac{1}{2} \right)\underset{\sim}{a} + \left( \frac{1}{2} \right)\underset{\sim}{b}$$

9.  $PQR$ is a triangle. The point $M$ is the midpoint of $QR$.

    a.  ![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image89.png){width="1.2349037620297463in"
        height="1.448167104111986in"}Write $\overrightarrow{QM}$ in
        terms of $\underset{\sim}{a}$ and $\underset{\sim}{b}$.

> $$- \underset{\sim}{b}$$

b.  Write $\overrightarrow{PM}$ in terms of $\underset{\sim}{a}$ and
    $\underset{\sim}{b}$.

> $$\underset{\sim}{a} + \underset{\sim}{3b}$$

10. $ABC$ is a triangle. The point $M$ is the midpoint of $BA$.

    a.  ![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image90.png){width="2.753967629046369in"
        height="1.4208267716535432in"}Write $\overrightarrow{BM}$ in
        terms of $\underset{\sim}{a}$ and $\underset{\sim}{b}$.

> $$\frac{3}{2}\underset{\sim}{a} - \underset{\sim}{2b}$$

b.  Write $\overrightarrow{CM}$ in terms of $\underset{\sim}{a}$ and
    $\underset{\sim}{b}.$

> $$- \frac{1}{2}\underset{\sim}{a} - \underset{\sim}{b}$$

11. $ABCD$ is a trapezium.
    $\overrightarrow{AD}\  = \ 3\overrightarrow{BC}$.

> ![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image91.png){width="2.2145319335083116in"
> height="1.2971019247594051in"}Write the vectors in terms of
> $\underset{\sim}{a}$ and $\underset{\sim}{b}.$

a.  $\overrightarrow{AD}$

> $$3\underset{\sim}{a}\  + 3\underset{\sim}{b}$$

b.  $\overrightarrow{AC}$

> $$2\underset{\sim}{a} + 5\underset{\sim}{b}$$

c.  $\overrightarrow{AB}$

> $$\underset{\sim}{a} + 4\underset{\sim}{b}$$

12. $\overrightarrow{OA}\  = \underset{\sim}{x},$
    $\overrightarrow{OB}\  = \underset{\sim}{y}.$

$N$ is the point on $AB$ such that $AN\ :\ NB\  = \ 1\ :\ 3$.

![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image92.png){width="2.3623195538057744in"
height="1.2589457567804025in"}Write $\overrightarrow{ON}$ in terms of
$\underset{\sim}{x}$ and $\underset{\sim}{y}.$

$$\frac{3}{4}\underset{\sim}{x} + \frac{1}{4}\underset{\sim}{y}$$

Development

13. $\overrightarrow{AB}\  = 4\underset{\sim}{a} + 6\underset{\sim}{b}$
    and
    $\overrightarrow{CD}\  = \ 10\underset{\sim}{a}\  + \ 15\underset{\sim}{b}.$

Show that $\overrightarrow{AB}$ and $\overrightarrow{CD}$ are parallel.

$$4\underset{\sim}{a}\  + \ 6\underset{\sim}{b}\  = \ 2\left( 2\underset{\sim}{a}\  + \ 3\underset{\sim}{b} \right)and\ \underset{\sim}{10a}\  + \ 15\underset{\sim}{b}\  = \ 5\left( 2\underset{\sim}{a}\  + \ 3\underset{\sim}{b} \right)\ $$

Both vectors are scalar multiples of
$\left( 2\underset{\sim}{a}\  + \ 3\underset{\sim}{b} \right)$ so they
are parallel.

14. $\overrightarrow{PQ}\  = \underset{\sim}{a}\  + \ 3\underset{\sim}{b}$
    and
    $\overrightarrow{RS}\  = \ 4\underset{\sim}{a}\  + \ 15\underset{\sim}{b}$.

Show that $\overrightarrow{PQ}$ and $\overrightarrow{RS}$ are not
parallel.

$${4\left( \underset{\sim}{a} + \underset{\sim}{3b} \right) = 4\underset{\sim}{a} + 12\underset{\sim}{b}
}{\neq 4\underset{\sim}{a} + 15\underset{\sim}{b}}$$

15. The triangle $ABC$ has vertices
    $A\left( 2\sqrt{3},\ 3 \right),\ B\left( 3\sqrt{3},\ 4 \right)\$and
    $C\left( 2\sqrt{3},\ 5 \right)$.

    a.  Find $\overrightarrow{AB}$ and $\overrightarrow{CB}$ as column
        vectors.

$${\overrightarrow{(OA)}\  = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
2\sqrt{3} \\
3
\end{array} \right)
}{\overrightarrow{OB}\  = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
3\sqrt{3} \\
4
\end{array} \right)
}{\overrightarrow{OC}\  = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
2\sqrt{3} \\
5
\end{array} \right)
}{\overrightarrow{AB}\  = \ \overrightarrow{OB}\  - \ \overrightarrow{OA}\  = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
3\sqrt{3} - \ 2\sqrt{3} \\
4\  - \ 3
\end{array} \right) = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
\sqrt{3} \\
1
\end{array} \right)
}{\overrightarrow{(CB)}\  = \ \overrightarrow{(OB)}\  - \ \overrightarrow{(OC)}\  = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
3\sqrt{3} - \ 2\sqrt{3} \\
4\  - \ 5
\end{array} \right) = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
\sqrt{3} \\
 - 1
\end{array} \right)}$$

b.  Hence find $\left| \overrightarrow{AB} \right|$ and
    $\left| \overrightarrow{CB} \right|$.

$${|\overrightarrow{AB}|\  = \ \sqrt{\left( \sqrt{3} \right)^{2} + \ 1^{2}}
}{= \ 2
}{|\overrightarrow{CB}|\  = \ \sqrt{\left( \sqrt{3} \right)^{2} + \ ( - 1)^{2}}
}{= \ 2}$$

c.  What type of special triangle is ABC? Why?

$${\overrightarrow{(AC)}\  = \ \overrightarrow{(OC)}\  - \ \overrightarrow{(OA)}\  = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
2\sqrt{3} - \ 2\sqrt{3} \\
5\  - \ 3
\end{array} \right) = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
0 \\
2
\end{array} \right)
}{|\overrightarrow{AC}|\  = \ \sqrt{0^{2} + \ 2^{2}}
}{= \ 2
}{|\overrightarrow{AB}|\  = \ |\overrightarrow{CB}|\  = \ |\overrightarrow{AC}|
}{Hence,\ ABC\ is\ an\ equilateral\ triangle\ as\ each\ side\ has\ a\ length\ of\ 2.}$$

16. The quadrilateral $ABCD$ has
    vertices$\ A( - 7,\  - 5),\ B(8,\  - 2),\ C(10,\ 9)$ and
    $D( - 5,\ 6).$\
    Show that $\overrightarrow{AD}\  = \ \overrightarrow{BC}$, and hence
    explain why $ABCD$ is a parallelogram.

$${\overrightarrow{AD}\  = \ \overrightarrow{OD}\  - \ \overrightarrow{OA}\  = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
 - 5\  - \ ( - 7) \\
6\  - \ ( - 5)
\end{array} \right) = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
2 \\
11
\end{array} \right)
}{\overrightarrow{BC}\  = \ \overrightarrow{OC}\  - \ \overrightarrow{OB}\  = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
10\  - \ 8 \\
9\  - \ ( - 2)
\end{array} \right) = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
2 \\
11
\end{array} \right)
}{Hence,\ \overrightarrow{AD}\  = \ \overrightarrow{BC}.
}{Therefore,\ ABCD\ is\ a\ parallelogram\ as\ opposite\ sides\ are\ equal\ and\ parallel.}$$

17. The quadrilateral PQRS has vertices
    $P( - 3,\  - 4),\ Q(2,\  - 2),\ R(4,\ 3)$ and $S( - 1,\ 1)$.

    a.  Show that $\overrightarrow{PQ}\  = \ \overrightarrow{SR}$.

$${\overrightarrow{PQ}\  = \ \overrightarrow{OQ}\  - \ \overrightarrow{OP}\  = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
2\  - \ ( - 3) \\
 - 2\  - \ ( - 4)
\end{array} \right) = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
5 \\
2
\end{array} \right)
}{\overrightarrow{SR}\  = \ \overrightarrow{OR}\  - \ \overrightarrow{OS}\  = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
4\  - \ ( - 1) \\
3\  - \ 1
\end{array} \right) = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
5 \\
2
\end{array} \right)
}{Hence,\ \overrightarrow{(PQ)}\  = \ \overrightarrow{(SR)}}$$

b.  Show that $|\overrightarrow{PQ}|\  = \ |\overrightarrow{QR}|.$

$${|\overrightarrow{PQ}|\  = \ \sqrt{5^{2} + \ 2^{2}}
}{= \ \sqrt{29}
}{\overrightarrow{QR}\  = \ \overrightarrow{OR}\  - \ \overrightarrow{OQ}\  = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
4\  - \ 2 \\
3\  - \ ( - 2)
\end{array} \right) = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
2 \\
5
\end{array} \right)
}{\left| \overrightarrow{QR} \right| = \ \sqrt{2^{2} + \ 5^{2}}
}{= \ \sqrt{29}
}{Hence,\ |\overrightarrow{PQ}|\  = \ |\overrightarrow{QR}|.}$$

c.  What type of special quadrilateral is $PQRS$? Give reasons for your
    answer.

PQRS is a rhombus as opposite sides are parallel and adjacent sides are
equal in length.

18. The parallelogram $WXYZ$ has vertices
    $W( - 6,\ 4),\ X(6,\ 2),\ Y(4,\ 9)\$and $Z(a,\ b).$\
    Use vectors to find the values of $a$ and $b$.

$${\overrightarrow{WZ}\  = \ \overrightarrow{OZ}\  - \ \overrightarrow{OW}\  = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
a\  - \ ( - 6) \\
b\  - \ 4
\end{array} \right) = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
a\  + \ 6 \\
b\  - \ 4
\end{array} \right)\ 
}{\overrightarrow{XY}\  = \ \overrightarrow{OY}\  - \ \overrightarrow{OX}\  = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
4\  - \ 6 \\
9\  - \ 2
\end{array} \right) = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
 - 2 \\
7
\end{array} \right)
}{As\ WXYZ\ is\ a\ parallelogram,\ opposite\ sides\ are\ equal\ hence,
}{\overrightarrow{WZ}\  = \ \overrightarrow{XY}
}{\left( \begin{array}{r}
a\  + \ 6 \\
b\  - \ 4
\end{array} \right) = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
 - 2 \\
7
\end{array} \right)
}{a\  + \ 6\  = \  - 2
}{a\  = \  - 8
}{b\  - \ 4\  = \ 7
}{b\  = \ 11}$$

19. The quadrilateral $OABC$ has vertices
    $O(0,\ 0),\ A(5,\  - 3),\ B(7,\ 4)$ and $C(2,\ 7).$

    a.  Show that
        $\overrightarrow{OA}\  + \frac{1}{2}\overrightarrow{AC}\  = \frac{1}{2}\overrightarrow{OB}$.

$${\overrightarrow{AC}\  = \ \overrightarrow{OC}\  - \ \overrightarrow{OA}\  = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
2\  - \ 5 \\
7\  - \ ( - 3)
\end{array} \right) = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
 - 3 \\
10
\end{array} \right)
}{\frac{1}{2}\overrightarrow{(AC)}\  = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
 - \frac{3}{2} \\
5
\end{array} \right)
}{\overrightarrow{OA}\  + \frac{1}{2}\overrightarrow{AC} = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
5 \\
 - 3
\end{array} \right) + \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
 - \frac{3}{2} \\
5
\end{array} \right)
}{= \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
\frac{7}{2} \\
2
\end{array} \right)
}{\overrightarrow{OB}\  = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
7 \\
4
\end{array} \right)
}{\frac{1}{2}\overrightarrow{OB}\  = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
\frac{7}{2} \\
2
\end{array} \right)}$$

b.  What type of special quadrilateral is $OABC$? Give a reason for your
    answer.

$\overrightarrow{OA}\  + \frac{1}{2}\overrightarrow{AC}$ is the vector
from the origin to the midpoint of diagonal AC

$\frac{1}{2}\overrightarrow{OB}$ is the vector from the origin to the
midpoint of diagonal OB

Since the midpoints of the diagonals are equal,

OABC is a parallelogram as diagonals bisect each other.

20. ![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image93.png){width="2.6506944444444445in"
    height="1.3694444444444445in"}ABC is a triangle.

    a.  Write $\overrightarrow{AC}$ in terms of $\underset{\sim}{a}$ and
        $\underset{\sim}{b}.$

> $$4\underset{\sim}{a} - 2\underset{\sim}{b}$$

b.  Show that $\overrightarrow{AC}$ is parallel to
    $2\underset{\sim}{a}\  - \underset{\sim}{b}.$

> $$4\underset{\sim}{a} - 2\underset{\sim}{b} = \ 2\left( 2\underset{\sim}{a} - \underset{\sim}{b} \right)$$

21. PQR is a triangle.

![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image94.png){width="2.0579713473315837in"
height="1.7660214348206473in"}Show that $\overrightarrow{PR}$ is
parallel to $\underset{\sim}{a}\  - \underset{\sim}{b}.$

$$\overrightarrow{PR} = \underset{\sim}{4a} - \underset{\sim}{4b} = \ 4\left( \underset{\sim}{a} - \underset{\sim}{b} \right)$$

22. $OAB$ is a triangle. $M$ is the midpoint of $OA$. $N$ is the
    midpoint of $OB$.

$\overrightarrow{OM}\  = \underset{\sim}{a},\ \overrightarrow{ON}\  = \underset{\sim}{b}.$

Show that $AB$ is parallel to $MN$.

![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image95.png){width="2.526421697287839in"
height="1.6811603237095363in"}

$\overrightarrow{AB}\  = \  - 2\underset{\sim}{a} + 2\underset{\sim}{b},\$\
$\overrightarrow{MN}\  = \  - \underset{\sim}{a} + \underset{\sim}{b},\$\
$\overrightarrow{AB}\  = \  - \underset{\sim}{2a} + \underset{\sim}{2b} = \ 2\left( - \underset{\sim}{a} + \underset{\sim}{b} \right)\$

23. $\overrightarrow{AC}\  = \ 2\underset{\sim}{a}\  - \ 3\underset{\sim}{b}$
    and
    $\overrightarrow{BC}\  = 8\underset{\sim}{a}\  - 12\underset{\sim}{b}.$

Prove that the points A, B and C lie on a straight line.

$$\overrightarrow{BC} = 8\underset{\sim}{a} - 12\underset{\sim}{b} = \ 4\left( 2\underset{\sim}{a} - 3\underset{\sim}{b} \right).\ $$

$$\overrightarrow{BC}\ and\ \overrightarrow{AC}\ are\ parallel.\ C\ is\ a\ common\ point.$$

$$\therefore collinear$$

24. a.  Test whether the points P, Q and R are collinear if

$$\overrightarrow{OP}\  = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
 - 1 \\
6
\end{array} \right)\ and\ \overrightarrow{OQ}\  = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
3 \\
2
\end{array} \right)\ and\ \overrightarrow{OR}\  = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
8 \\
 - 3
\end{array} \right).$$

$${\overrightarrow{PQ}\  = \ \overrightarrow{OQ}\  - \ \overrightarrow{OP}
}{= \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
3\  - \ ( - 1) \\
2\  - \ 6
\end{array} \right)
}{= \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
4 \\
 - 4
\end{array} \right)
}{Gradient\  = \frac{y}{x} = \frac{- 4}{4} = \  - 1}$$

$$
{\overrightarrow{QR}\  = \ \overrightarrow{OR}\  - \ \overrightarrow{OQ}
}{\ \ \ \ \ \ \ \  = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
8\  - \ 3 \\
( - 3) - \ 2
\end{array} \right)
}{\ \ \ \ \ \ \ \  = \ \left( \begin{array}{r}
5 \\
 - 5
\end{array} \right)
}{Gradient\  = \frac{y}{x} = \frac{- 5}{5} = \  - 1
}
{P,\ Q\ and\ R\ are\ collinear\ as\ the\ gradient\ is\  - 1.}$$

b.  Test whether the points A, B and C are collinear if
