$$\overrightarrow{OA}\  = \ 3\mathbf{i}\  + \ 8\mathbf{j\ }and\ \overrightarrow{OB}\  = \  - \mathbf{i}\  + \ 3\mathbf{j}\ and\ \overrightarrow{OC}\  = \  - 4\mathbf{i}\  - \ \mathbf{j}.$$

$${\overrightarrow{AB}\  = \ \overrightarrow{OB}\  - \ \overrightarrow{OA}
}{= \ \left( - \mathbf{i}\  + \ 3\mathbf{j} \right) - \ \left( 3\mathbf{i}\  + \ 8\mathbf{j} \right)
}{= \  - 4\mathbf{i}\  - \ 5\mathbf{j}
}{Gradient\  = \frac{y}{x} = \frac{- 5}{- 4} = \frac{5}{4}}$$

$${\overrightarrow{BC}\  = \ \overrightarrow{OC}\  - \ \overrightarrow{OB}
}{\ \ \ \ \ \ \ \  = \ ( - 4\mathbf{i}\  - \ \mathbf{j})\  - \ \left( - \mathbf{i}\  + \ 3\mathbf{j} \right)
}{\ \ \ \ \ \ \ \  = \  - 3\mathbf{i}\  - \ 4\mathbf{j}
}{Gradient\  = \frac{y}{x} = \frac{- 3}{- 4} = \frac{3}{4}\ 
}
{A,\ B\ and\ C\ are\ not\ collinear\ as\ the\ gradient\ is\ not\ same.}$$

25. In △ABC, the point M is the midpoint of BC.\
    If $\overrightarrow{AC}\  = \ \overset{̰}{u}$ and
    $\overrightarrow{BC}\  = \ \overset{̰}{v}$, write in terms of
    $\overset{̰}{u}$ and $\overset{̰}{v}$:

    a.  $\overrightarrow{AB}$

$${\overrightarrow{AB} = \overrightarrow{AC} + \overrightarrow{CB}
}{= \overrightarrow{AC} - \overrightarrow{BC}
}{= \ \overset{̰}{u}\  - \ \overset{̰}{v}}$$

b.  $\overrightarrow{AM}$

$${\overrightarrow{AM} = \overrightarrow{AC} + \overrightarrow{CM}
}{= \overrightarrow{AC} - \overrightarrow{MC}
}{= \overrightarrow{AC} - \frac{1}{2}\overrightarrow{BC}
}{= \ \overset{̰}{u}\  - \frac{1}{2}\overset{̰}{v}}$$

26. ![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image96.png){width="1.5748031496062993in"
    height="1.7151749781277341in"}In the diagram, ABCD is a
    quadrilateral and M is the midpoint of BC.\
    Let $\overrightarrow{CD}\  = \ \overset{̰}{u},$
    $\overrightarrow{BA}\  = \ \overset{̰}{v}$ and
    $\overrightarrow{AD}\  = \ \overset{̰}{w}$.

    a.  Find $\overrightarrow{MB}$ in terms of $\overset{̰}{u}$,
        $\overset{̰}{v}$ and $\overset{̰}{w}$.

$${\overrightarrow{AC}\  = \ \overrightarrow{AD}\  + \ \overrightarrow{DC}
}{= \ \overrightarrow{AD}\  - \ \overrightarrow{CD}
}{= \ \overset{̰}{w}\  - \ \overset{̰}{u}
}{\overrightarrow{BC}\  = \ \overrightarrow{BA}\  + \ \overrightarrow{AC}\  = \ \overset{̰}{v} + \ \overset{̰}{w} - \ \overset{̰}{u}
}{As\ M\ is\ the\ midpoint\ of\ BC,
}{\overrightarrow{BM}\  = \frac{1}{2}\overrightarrow{BC}\  = \frac{1}{2}\left( \overset{̰}{v} + \ \overset{̰}{w} - \ \overset{̰}{u} \right)
}{\overrightarrow{MB}\ is\ in\ opposite\ direction\ of\ \overrightarrow{BM}.
}{Hence,\ \overrightarrow{MB}\  = \  - \frac{1}{2}\left( \overset{̰}{v} + \ \overset{̰}{w} - \ \overset{̰}{u} \right)\  = \frac{1}{2}\left( \overset{̰}{u} - \ \overset{̰}{v} - \ \overset{̰}{w} \right)}$$

b.  Hence find $\overrightarrow{MA}$ in terms of
    $\overset{̰}{u},\ \overset{̰}{v}$ and $\overset{̰}{w}$.

$${\overrightarrow{MA}\  = \ \overrightarrow{MB}\  + \ \overrightarrow{BA}
}{= \ \left( \frac{1}{2} \right)\left( \underset{\sim}{u} - \underset{\sim}{v} - \underset{\sim}{w} \right) + \underset{\sim}{v}
}{\overrightarrow{MA}\  = \frac{1}{2}\left( \overset{̰}{u} + \ \overset{̰}{v} - \ \overset{̰}{w} \right)}$$

27. Suppose that P is a point on the line AB such that
    $\overrightarrow{AP}\  = \ k\overrightarrow{AB}$, where $k$ is a
    constant.

    a.  Show on a diagram the position of P relative to A and B if:

+---------------------+-----------------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| i.  $k\  > 1\$      | ii. $0 < \ k\  < \ 1$ | iii. ![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image97.png){width="1.4555555555555555in"                               |
|                     |                       |      height="1.0611111111111111in"}![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image98.png){width="1.4645833333333333in" |
|                     |                       |      height="1.0486111111111112in"}![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image99.png){width="1.414776902887139in"  |
|                     |                       |      height="1.0850010936132983in"}$k < \ 0$                                                                                                                          |
+=====================+=======================+=======================================================================================================================================================================+

a.  Find the value of $k$ for which:

+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| i.  $\overrightarrow{AP}\  = \frac{3}{4}\overrightarrow{PB}$                                       | ii. $\overrightarrow{AP}\  = \  - \frac{3}{5}\overrightarrow{PB}$                                         | iii. $\overrightarrow{AP}\  = \  - \frac{2}{3}\overrightarrow{PB}$                                     |
|                                                                                                    |                                                                                                           |                                                                                                        |
| $${\overrightarrow{AP}\  = \frac{3}{4}\left( \overrightarrow{AB}\  - \ \overrightarrow{AP} \right) | $${\overrightarrow{AP}\  = \  - \frac{3}{5}\left( \overrightarrow{AB}\  - \ \overrightarrow{AP} \right)\  | $${\overrightarrow{AP}\  = \  - \frac{2}{3}\overrightarrow{PB}                                         |
| }{\overrightarrow{AP}\  = \frac{3}{4}\ \overrightarrow{AB}\  - \frac{3}{4}\overrightarrow{AP}      | }{\overrightarrow{AP}\  = \  - \frac{3}{5}\overrightarrow{AB}\  + \frac{3}{5}\overrightarrow{AP}          | }{\overrightarrow{PB}\  = \ \overrightarrow{AB}\  - \ \overrightarrow{AP}                              |
| }{\frac{7}{4}\overrightarrow{AP}\  = \frac{3}{4}\overrightarrow{AB}                                | }{\frac{2}{5}\overrightarrow{AP}\  = \  - \frac{3}{5}\overrightarrow{AB}                                  | }{\overrightarrow{AP}\  = \  - \frac{2}{3}\left( \overrightarrow{AB}\  - \ \overrightarrow{AP} \right) |
| }{\frac{7}{4}k\overrightarrow{AB}\  = \frac{3}{4}\overrightarrow{AB}                               | }{\frac{2}{5}k\overrightarrow{AB}\  = \  - \frac{3}{5}\overrightarrow{AB}                                 | }{\overrightarrow{AP}\  = \  - \frac{2}{3}\overrightarrow{AB}\  + \frac{2}{3}\ \overrightarrow{AP}     |
| }{k\  = \frac{3}{7}}$$                                                                             | }{k\  = \  - \frac{3}{2}}$$                                                                               | }{\frac{1}{3}\overrightarrow{AP}\  = \  - \frac{2}{3}\ \overrightarrow{AB}                             |
|                                                                                                    |                                                                                                           | }{\frac{1}{3}\ k\overrightarrow{AB}\  = \  - \frac{2}{3}\overrightarrow{AB}                            |
|                                                                                                    |                                                                                                           | }{k\  = \  - 2}$$                                                                                      |


a.  Let $\overset{̰}{a}$, $\overset{̰}{b}$ and $\overset{̰}{p}\$ be the
    respective position vectors of the points A, B and P relative to an
    origin O. Prove that
    $\overset{̰}{p} = \ (1\  - \ k)\overset{̰}{a} + \ k\overset{̰}{b}$.

$${\overrightarrow{AB}\  = \ \ \overrightarrow{OB}\  - \ \overrightarrow{OA}\  = \ \overset{̰}{b}\ \  - \ \overset{̰}{a}
}{and,\ \ \ \overrightarrow{AP}\  = \ \ \overrightarrow{OP}\  - \ \overrightarrow{OA}\  = \ \overset{̰}{p}\  - \ \overset{̰}{a}\ 
}{Given,\ \overrightarrow{AP}\  = \ k\overrightarrow{AB}
}{\overset{̰}{p} - \ \overset{̰}{a} = \ k\left( \overset{̰}{b} - \ \overset{̰}{a} \right)\ 
}{\overset{̰}{p} - \ \overset{̰}{a} = \ k\overset{̰}{b} - \ k\overset{̰}{a}
}{\overset{̰}{p} = \ \overset{̰}{a} - \ k\overset{̰}{a} + \ k\overset{̰}{b} = \ (1\  - \ k)\overset{̰}{a} + \ k\overset{̰}{b}}$$

28. ![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image100.png){width="1.6535433070866141in"
    height="1.786745406824147in"}M and N are the midpoints of AB and
    PQ.\
    Let $\overset{̰}{u} = \ \overrightarrow{AM}$,
    $\overset{̰}{v} = \ \overrightarrow{PN},\ \overset{̰}{a} = \ \overrightarrow{AP},\ \overset{̰}{b} = \ \overrightarrow{BQ}$
    and $\overset{̰}{p} = \ \overrightarrow{MN}$.

    a.  Explain why $\overrightarrow{MB} = \overset{̰}{u}$ and
        $\overrightarrow{NQ}\  = \ \overset{̰}{v}$.

$\overrightarrow{AM}$ and $\overrightarrow{MB}$ have the same length and
direction.

Similarly, $\overrightarrow{PN}$ and $\overrightarrow{NQ}$ have the same
length and direction.

b.  Find $\overset{̰}{a}$ and $\overset{̰}{b}$ in terms of
    $\overset{̰}{p},\ \overset{̰}{u}$ and$\ \overset{̰}{v}$.

$${\overrightarrow{AN}\  = \ \overrightarrow{AP}\  + \ \overrightarrow{PN}\  = \ \overset{̰}{a}\  + \ \overset{̰}{v}
}{and\ \overrightarrow{AN}\  = \ \overrightarrow{AM}\  + \ \overrightarrow{MN}\  = \ \overset{̰}{u}\  + \ \overset{̰}{p}
}{Hence,
}{\overset{̰}{a}\  + \ \overset{̰}{v}\  = \ \overset{̰}{u}\  + \ \overset{̰}{p}
}{\overset{̰}{a} = \ \overset{̰}{p} + \ \overset{̰}{u} - \ \overset{̰}{v},
}{Similarly,\ \ \ \overrightarrow{MQ}\  = \ \overrightarrow{MB}\  + \ \overrightarrow{BQ}\  = \ \overset{̰}{u}\  + \ \overset{̰}{b}
}{and\ \overrightarrow{MQ}\  = \ \overrightarrow{MN}\  + \ \overrightarrow{NQ}\  = \ \overset{̰}{p} + \ \overset{̰}{v}
}{Hence,
}{\overset{̰}{u}\ \  + \ \overset{̰}{b} = \ \overset{̰}{p} + \ \overset{̰}{v}
}{\overset{̰}{b} = \ \overset{̰}{p} - \ \overset{̰}{u} + \ \overset{̰}{v}}$$

c.  Hence show that
    $\overset{̰}{a} + \ \overset{̰}{b} = \ 2\overset{̰}{p}$.

$${\overset{̰}{a} + \ \overset{̰}{b} = \ \overset{̰}{p} + \ \overset{̰}{u} - \ \overset{̰}{v} + \ \left( \overset{̰}{p} - \ \overset{̰}{u} + \ \overset{̰}{v} \right)
}{= \ 2\overset{̰}{p}}$$

29. $\overrightarrow{NM}\  = \ 4\underset{\sim}{b}$ and
    $\overrightarrow{JM}\  = \underset{\sim}{a}.$

    a.  ![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image101.png){width="3.729755030621172in"
        height="0.9057972440944881in"}Write $\overrightarrow{NJ}$ in
        terms of $\underset{\sim}{a}$ and $\underset{\sim}{b}.$

$$- \underset{\sim}{a}\  + \ 4\underset{\sim}{b}$$

N, J and K are collinear.
$\overrightarrow{NK}\  = \ 3\  \times \ \overrightarrow{NJ}$.

b.  Write $\overrightarrow{NK}$ in terms of $\underset{\sim}{a}$ and
    $\underset{\sim}{b}.$

$$- 3\underset{\sim}{a}\  + \ 12\underset{\sim}{b}$$

c.  Write $\overrightarrow{MK}$ in terms of $\underset{\sim}{a}$ and
    $\underset{\sim}{b}$.

$$- 3\underset{\sim}{a} + 8\underset{\sim}{b}$$

Mastery

30. ![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image102.png){width="3.044082458442695in"
    height="1.4394225721784777in"}ABC is a triangle.
    $\overrightarrow{AB}\  = \underset{\sim}{a}$ and
    $\overrightarrow{AC}\  = 2\underset{\sim}{b}.$

M is the midpoint of AB.

N is the point on BC such that $BN\ :\ NC\  = \ 3\ :\ 1$.

D is the point such that
$\overrightarrow{CD}\  = \frac{1}{2}\overrightarrow{AC}$.

a.  Write the vector $\overrightarrow{MN}$ in terms of
    $\underset{\sim}{a}$ and $\underset{\sim}{b}.$

$$- \frac{1}{4}\underset{\sim}{a}\  + \frac{3}{2}\underset{\sim}{b}$$

b.  Use vectors to prove that M, N and D lie on the same straight line.

$${\overrightarrow{MN}\  = \  - \frac{1}{4}\underset{\sim}{a}\  + \frac{3}{2}\underset{\sim}{b}\  = \  - \frac{1}{4}\left( \underset{\sim}{a} - \underset{\sim}{6b} \right)
}{\overrightarrow{MD}\  = \  - \frac{1}{2}\underset{\sim}{a}\  + \ 3\underset{\sim}{b}\  = \  - \frac{1}{2}\left( \underset{\sim}{a} - \underset{\sim}{6b} \right)
}{\overrightarrow{MN}\ and\ \overrightarrow{MD}\ are\ parallel\ and\ share\ a\ common\ point\ M.
}{\therefore collinear}$$

31. ![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image103.png){width="3.170984251968504in"
    height="2.0173053368328957in"}PQR is a triangle with
    $\underset{\sim}{x},\underset{\sim}{y}$ and $2\underset{\sim}{y}$ as
    shown.

N is a point on QP, such that QN = 2NP.

R is the midpoint of QS.

M is the midpoint of PR.

a.  Write $\overrightarrow{PR}$ in terms of $\underset{\sim}{x}$ and
    $\underset{\sim}{y}.$

$$- 3\underset{\sim}{y} + \underset{\sim}{x}$$

b.  Write $\overrightarrow{NM}$ in terms of $\underset{\sim}{x}$ and
    $\underset{\sim}{y}.$

$$- \frac{1}{2}\underset{\sim}{y}\  + \frac{1}{2}\underset{\sim}{x}$$

c.  Show that NMS is a straight line.

$${\overrightarrow{NS}\  = \  - 2\underset{\sim}{y}\  + \ 2\underset{\sim}{x}\  = \  - 2\left( \underset{\sim}{y} - \underset{\sim}{x} \right)
}{\overrightarrow{NM}\  = \  - \frac{1}{2}\underset{\sim}{y}\  + \frac{1}{2}\underset{\sim}{x}\  = \  - \frac{1}{2}\left( \underset{\sim}{y} - \underset{\sim}{x} \right)
}{\overrightarrow{NM}\ and\ \overrightarrow{NS}\ are\ parallel\ and\ share\ a\ common\ point\ N.
}{\therefore collinear}$$

32. ![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image104.png){width="4.062357830271216in"
    height="1.8946609798775154in"}OAB is a triangle.

A is the midpoint of OC.

N is the midpoint of AB.

M is on OB such that $OM\ :\ MB\  = \ 2\ :\ 1.$

$\overrightarrow{NB}\  = \underset{\sim}{x}$,
$\overrightarrow{OA}\  = \underset{\sim}{y}.$

Prove that MNC is a straight line.

$${\overrightarrow{MN}\  = \frac{1}{3}\left( 2\underset{\sim}{x} + \underset{\sim}{y} \right)\  - \underset{\sim}{x}
}{\  = \  - \frac{1}{3}\left( \underset{\sim}{x} - \underset{\sim}{y} \right)
}{\overrightarrow{MC}\  = \  - \frac{4}{3}\underset{\sim}{x}\  + \frac{4}{3}\underset{\sim}{y}\ 
}{= \  - \frac{4}{3}\left( \underset{\sim}{x} - \underset{\sim}{y} \right)
}{\overrightarrow{MN}\ and\ \overrightarrow{MC}\ are\ parallel\ and\ share\ a\ common\ point\ M.}$$

![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image105.png){width="3.0027777777777778in"
height="1.9949354768153982in"}

33. $ABD$ is a triangle.

$N$ is a point on $AD$ such that $AN\ :\ ND\  = \ 2\ :\ 1.$

a.  Write $\overrightarrow{DB}$ in terms of
    $\underset{\sim}{a}\ and\ \underset{\sim}{b}.$

$$\overrightarrow{DB}\  = \  - 3\underset{\sim}{b}\  + \underset{\sim}{a}$$

$B$ is the midpoint of $AC$.

$M$ is the midpoint of $BD$.

b.  Show that NMC is a straight line.

$$\overrightarrow{NM}\  = \frac{1}{2}\left( - \underset{\sim}{b} + \underset{\sim}{a} \right),\ \overrightarrow{NC} = 2\left( - \underset{\sim}{b} + \underset{\sim}{a} \right)$$

Therefore, parallel and share a common point N.

34. ![](media/Introduction to Vectors 1_Vector Representation, notation and operations/media/image106.png){width="2.188888888888889in"
    height="2.1773086176727907in"}The triangle $PQR$ is shown. $M$ is
    the midpoint of $QR$.

$\overrightarrow{PR}\  = \underset{\sim}{x},\ \overrightarrow{PQ}\  = \underset{\sim}{y}.$

$\overrightarrow{PO}\ :\ \overrightarrow{OM}\  = \ 3\ :\ 2.$

$\overrightarrow{PN}$ can be written in the form $k\underset{\sim}{x}.$

Work out the value of $k$.

$${\overrightarrow{PR}\  = \underset{\sim}{x},\ \overrightarrow{PQ}\  = \underset{\sim}{y}\ 
}{\overrightarrow{QR}\  = \underset{\sim}{x} - \underset{\sim}{y}
}{\overrightarrow{QM}\  = \frac{1}{2}\left( \underset{\sim}{x} - \underset{\sim}{y} \right)\ }$$

$${\overrightarrow{PQ}\  + \ \overrightarrow{QM}\  = \ \overrightarrow{PM}
}{\underset{\sim}{y} + \frac{1}{2}\left( \underset{\sim}{x} - \underset{\sim}{y} \right) = \ \overrightarrow{PM}\ 
}{\therefore\ \overrightarrow{PM}\  = \frac{1}{2}\left( \underset{\sim}{x} + \underset{\sim}{y} \right)}$$

$${\overrightarrow{PO}\  = \frac{3}{5}\left( \underset{\sim}{\frac{1}{2}x} + \underset{\sim}{y} \right)\  = \frac{3}{10}\left( \underset{\sim}{x} + \underset{\sim}{y} \right)
}{\overrightarrow{PO}\  = \ \overrightarrow{PQ}\  + \ \overrightarrow{QO}
}{\overrightarrow{PO}\  = \ \overrightarrow{PQ}\  + \ m\  \times \ \overrightarrow{QN}
}{\frac{3}{10}\left( \underset{\sim}{x} + \underset{\sim}{y} \right)\  = \underset{\sim}{y} + \ m\left( \underset{\sim}{kx} - \underset{\sim}{y} \right)
}{\therefore\frac{3}{10}\underset{\sim}{x} + \frac{3}{10}\underset{\sim}{y} = \ mk\underset{\sim}{x} + \ (1 - m)\underset{\sim}{y}\ \ }$$

Comparing coefficients:

$${mk\  = \frac{3}{10}
}{1 - m\  = \frac{3}{10}
}{m\  = \frac{7}{10}
}{\therefore\ k\  = \frac{3}{7}}$$
